'''久米氏'''（くめうじ）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]の一つ。 == 久米直 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏
（久米直） |image = |氏姓 = 久米直 |始祖 = 味耳命（また味日命とも） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = [[山部氏|山部]][[連]] }} 古代日本における軍事氏族の一つで、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では[[高魂命|高御魂命]]の8世の孫である味耳命（うましみみのみこと）の後裔とも、[[神魂命]]の8世の孫である味日命（うましひのみこと）の後裔とも伝わり、[[久米部]]（「くめべ」と読む。来目部とも表記することもある）の[[伴造]]氏族（[[久味国造]]）。 『[[日本書紀]]』神代下天孫降臨章1書には、[[大伴氏]]の遠祖の[[天忍日命]]が、来目部の遠祖である天槵津大来目（[[天津久米命]]）を率いて[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]を先導して天降ったと記されており、『新撰姓氏録』左京神別中の大伴宿禰条にも同様の記述がある。このことから、久米[[直 (姓)|直]]・久米部は大伴氏の配下にあって軍事的役割を有していたと考えられている。 ただ、『[[古事記]]』には天忍日命と天津久米命の2人が太刀・弓矢などを持って[[天孫降臨]]に供奉したとあり、大伴氏と久米氏を対等の立場として扱っており、[[神武東征]]において[[道臣命]]と[[大久米命]]の間にも記紀で同様の相違が認められる。すなわち、久米部を統轄する一族がある時点で衰えたため、大伴氏がかわりに久米部を管理するようになったということである{{Sfn|坂本ほか|1994|pp=382-383}}。『古事記』の伝承は、その古い形を伝えるものだったのかも知れない。 また、神武天皇東征説話に見える[[久米歌|来目歌]]、戦闘歌舞の代表といえる[[久米舞]]は、久米氏・久米部の性格を考える上で重要である{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2158}}。 === 由来諸説 === クメの原義に関する考察は、古くは[[本居宣長]]説として、「人の目の円く大きく、くるくるしたるゆえ」から始まり（以下、後述論文を参考）、クメ＝クミ（組）説、「クメベは軍隊のムレ（群れ）」説（[[橘守部]]、[[飯田武郷]]）、クメ＝クマ（肥）であり、「肥人つまり異民族の部民」説（[[喜田貞吉]]）、クメ＝クベ（垣）で、「宮廷の御垣」説（[[折口信夫]]）、クメ＝西南島々の地名説、「大伴氏が元々来目であり、大伴の部民」説（[[高橋富雄]]）が挙げられる（後述論文 p.322.）。 [[上田正昭]]は、本居説は論外であり、クベ＝垣説には注意を引くものがあるとした上で、「久米歌から考察するに、[[宇陀郡|宇陀]]の高城の防御を施した垣の内の集団生活にこそ、久米集団の本源的様相があり、元は山人で、南大和の王権に直属するようになり、御県の神が祀られるようになった」とする上田正昭「戦闘歌舞の伝統」（上田正昭編 『論集 日本文化の起源 第2巻 日本史』平凡社、 1971年）pp.322 - 323.。 [[ja:久米氏]] == 久米臣 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏
（久米臣） |image = |氏姓 = 久米臣 |始祖 = |出自 = [[春日氏#和珥氏族（春日臣）|春日臣]] |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = [[Yamato Province ]][[高市郡]]久米郷 |人物 = |後裔 = }} [[Yamato Province ]][[高市郡]]久米郷より起こる。[[春日氏#和珥氏族（春日臣）|春日氏]]の傍流{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2158}}。 また、[[蘇我稲目]]の四男・[[蘇我刀名]]の子・[[久米薬子]]を祖とする久米臣も存在する倉本一宏『蘇我氏　古代豪族の興亡』（中央公論新社、2015年）。 {{-}} == 尾張国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏 |image = |氏姓 = 久米 |始祖 = 八甕命 |出自 = 久米直 |氏祖 = |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天神]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province ]][[高市郡]]久米郷 |人物 = |後裔 = }} 久米氏の族人七掬脛の子・八甕命の流れ。氷上神社祠官であるといわれる{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2159}}。 {{-}} == 武蔵国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]][[村山氏|村山支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[武蔵国]][[入間郡]]久米庄 |根拠地= 武蔵国入間郡久米庄 |人物= |支流= }} [[武蔵国]][[入間郡]][[久米庄]]より起こる。[[本姓]]を[[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]]とする[[村山氏]]の流れという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2160}}。 {{-}} == 常陸国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= 1.[[藤原北家]][[藤原秀郷|秀郷流]][[小野崎氏|小野崎氏流]]
2.[[清和源氏]][[源義光|義光流]][[佐竹氏|佐竹支流]] |家祖= [[佐竹義武|久米義武]] |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[常陸国]][[久慈郡]]久米郷 |根拠地= 常陸国久慈郡久米郷 |人物= |支流= }} [[常陸国]]に[[久慈郡]]久米郷より起こる久米氏がいた。[[藤原北家]][[小野崎氏]]流があり、小野崎通室（筑前入道）の代に、男子がなく同族の[[小貫氏]]一族の[[小貫頼重]]（[[和田昭為]]の子、[[小貫頼久]]の養子）の子の通治を養子に迎えた。後に[[清和源氏]][[佐竹氏]]の当主の[[佐竹義治]]の子[[佐竹義武|久米義武]]がその名跡を継ぐも、[[佐竹義知 (山入佐竹氏)|山入義知]]の攻撃で討たれると、弟の[[佐竹義信|義信]]がその後を継いだという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2161}}。 なお、[[幕末]]の[[水戸藩]][[家老]] [[安島帯刀]]の妻は、通治の末裔ともされる[[久米長重|久米新七郎長重]]の娘であり、久米氏との間に[[御三卿]]筆頭 [[一橋徳川家]][[中老]]となった長女、水戸藩定江戸小姓 [[立原朴二郎]]妻となる二女、[[安島七郎太郎|安島七郎太郎信義]]、[[安島七郎三郎]]などの子があった[[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻8.5丁、145丁。 {{-}} == 出羽国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（出羽国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[藤原氏]][[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂|武智麻呂系]][[二階堂氏|二階堂支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[出羽国]]山本郡（現[[仙北郡]]）[[飯詰村 (秋田県)|飯詰]] |根拠地= 出羽国仙北郡飯詰 |人物= |支流= }} revision.text:
'''久米氏'''（くめうじ）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]の一つ。 == 久米直 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏
（久米直） |image = |氏姓 = 久米直 |始祖 = 味耳命（また味日命とも） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = [[山部氏|山部]][[連]] }} 古代日本における軍事氏族の一つで、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では[[高魂命|高御魂命]]の8世の孫である味耳命（うましみみのみこと）の後裔とも、[[神魂命]]の8世の孫である味日命（うましひのみこと）の後裔とも伝わり、[[久米部]]（「くめべ」と読む。来目部とも表記することもある）の[[伴造]]氏族（[[久味国造]]）。 『[[日本書紀]]』神代下天孫降臨章1書には、[[大伴氏]]の遠祖の[[天忍日命]]が、来目部の遠祖である天槵津大来目（[[天津久米命]]）を率いて[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]を先導して天降ったと記されており、『新撰姓氏録』左京神別中の大伴宿禰条にも同様の記述がある。このことから、久米[[直 (姓)|直]]・久米部は大伴氏の配下にあって軍事的役割を有していたと考えられている。 ただ、『[[古事記]]』には天忍日命と天津久米命の2人が太刀・弓矢などを持って[[天孫降臨]]に供奉したとあり、大伴氏と久米氏を対等の立場として扱っており、[[神武東征]]において[[道臣命]]と[[大久米命]]の間にも記紀で同様の相違が認められる。すなわち、久米部を統轄する一族がある時点で衰えたため、大伴氏がかわりに久米部を管理するようになったということである{{Sfn|坂本ほか|1994|pp=382-383}}。『古事記』の伝承は、その古い形を伝えるものだったのかも知れない。 また、神武天皇東征説話に見える[[久米歌|来目歌]]、戦闘歌舞の代表といえる[[久米舞]]は、久米氏・久米部の性格を考える上で重要である{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2158}}。 === 由来諸説 === クメの原義に関する考察は、古くは[[本居宣長]]説として、「人の目の円く大きく、くるくるしたるゆえ」から始まり（以下、後述論文を参考）、クメ＝クミ（組）説、「クメベは軍隊のムレ（群れ）」説（[[橘守部]]、[[飯田武郷]]）、クメ＝クマ（肥）であり、「肥人つまり異民族の部民」説（[[喜田貞吉]]）、クメ＝クベ（垣）で、「宮廷の御垣」説（[[折口信夫]]）、クメ＝西南島々の地名説、「大伴氏が元々来目であり、大伴の部民」説（[[高橋富雄]]）が挙げられる（後述論文 p.322.）。 [[上田正昭]]は、本居説は論外であり、クベ＝垣説には注意を引くものがあるとした上で、「久米歌から考察するに、[[宇陀郡|宇陀]]の高城の防御を施した垣の内の集団生活にこそ、久米集団の本源的様相があり、元は山人で、南大和の王権に直属するようになり、御県の神が祀られるようになった」とする上田正昭「戦闘歌舞の伝統」（上田正昭編 『論集 日本文化の起源 第2巻 日本史』平凡社、 1971年）pp.322 - 323.。 [[ja:久米氏]] == 久米臣 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏
（久米臣） |image = |氏姓 = 久米臣 |始祖 = |出自 = [[春日氏#和珥氏族（春日臣）|春日臣]] |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = [[Yamato Province ]][[高市郡]]久米郷 |人物 = |後裔 = }} [[Yamato Province ]][[高市郡]]久米郷より起こる。[[春日氏#和珥氏族（春日臣）|春日氏]]の傍流{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2158}}。 また、[[蘇我稲目]]の四男・[[蘇我刀名]]の子・[[久米薬子]]を祖とする久米臣も存在する倉本一宏『蘇我氏　古代豪族の興亡』（中央公論新社、2015年）。 {{-}} == 尾張国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏 |image = |氏姓 = 久米 |始祖 = 八甕命 |出自 = 久米直 |氏祖 = |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天神]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province ]][[高市郡]]久米郷 |人物 = |後裔 = }} 久米氏の族人七掬脛の子・八甕命の流れ。氷上神社祠官であるといわれる{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2159}}。 {{-}} == 武蔵国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]][[村山氏|村山支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[武蔵国]][[入間郡]]久米庄 |根拠地= 武蔵国入間郡久米庄 |人物= |支流= }} [[武蔵国]][[入間郡]][[久米庄]]より起こる。[[本姓]]を[[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]]とする[[村山氏]]の流れという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2160}}。 {{-}} == 常陸国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= 1.[[藤原北家]][[藤原秀郷|秀郷流]][[小野崎氏|小野崎氏流]]
2.[[清和源氏]][[源義光|義光流]][[佐竹氏|佐竹支流]] |家祖= [[佐竹義武|久米義武]] |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[常陸国]][[久慈郡]]久米郷 |根拠地= 常陸国久慈郡久米郷 |人物= |支流= }} [[常陸国]]に[[久慈郡]]久米郷より起こる久米氏がいた。[[藤原北家]][[小野崎氏]]流があり、小野崎通室（筑前入道）の代に、男子がなく同族の[[小貫氏]]一族の[[小貫頼重]]（[[和田昭為]]の子、[[小貫頼久]]の養子）の子の通治を養子に迎えた。後に[[清和源氏]][[佐竹氏]]の当主の[[佐竹義治]]の子[[佐竹義武|久米義武]]がその名跡を継ぐも、[[佐竹義知 (山入佐竹氏)|山入義知]]の攻撃で討たれると、弟の[[佐竹義信|義信]]がその後を継いだという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2161}}。 なお、[[幕末]]の[[水戸藩]][[家老]] [[安島帯刀]]の妻は、通治の末裔ともされる[[久米長重|久米新七郎長重]]の娘であり、久米氏との間に[[御三卿]]筆頭 [[一橋徳川家]][[中老]]となった長女、水戸藩定江戸小姓 [[立原朴二郎]]妻となる二女、[[安島七郎太郎|安島七郎太郎信義]]、[[安島七郎三郎]]などの子があった[[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻8.5丁、145丁。 {{-}} == 出羽国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（出羽国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[藤原氏]][[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂|武智麻呂系]][[二階堂氏|二階堂支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[出羽国]]山本郡（現[[仙北郡]]）[[飯詰村 (秋田県)|飯詰]] |根拠地= 出羽国仙北郡飯詰 |人物= |支流= }} [[出羽国]]山本郡飯詰郷より起こった久米氏は、領主としては飯詰氏を名乗ることもあり、[[本姓]][[藤原氏]]、[[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂]]の子孫を称し、[[二階堂氏]]の分かれであるといわれている。[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の内乱期に出羽国に避難した一族が土着したと考えられる。[[曹洞宗]]の高僧[[道叟道愛]]も久米一族と伝わる。また、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、隣村[[六郷町 (秋田県)|六郷]]を本拠とした[[六郷氏]]も同族とみられ、久米姓は飯詰・六郷の所在する現在の[[美郷町 (秋田県)|美郷町]]やその周辺に濃密に分布し、また、六郷氏が出羽国[[由利郡]][[本荘藩]]の大名となったところから、その[[家臣]]として[[本荘市|本荘]]（現在の[[由利本荘市]]）へ随行した者、地元に残って[[秋田藩]][[家老]]渋江氏[[家臣]]の手前[[給人]]になった者r0403_katuhirajinja-sekizokomainu_1.pdfや[[飯詰村]]の[[豪農]]になった者[http://namahage.is.akita-u.ac.jp/monogatari/show_detail.php?serial_no=6152 与惣左エ門神社（よそうざえもんじんじゃ)]もおり、現在では[[秋田県]]南部から中部にかけて広く分布している。 {{-}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|ref=harv}} {{フリーアクセス}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|authorlink=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大伴氏}} * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞}} * {{ill|Kume District|ja|久米郡}} * revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大伴氏}} * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞}} * {{ill|Kume District|ja|久米郡}} * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和}} == Categories == [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Clans]] [[Category:Clans of Japan]] [[Category:Clans in the Kinki Region]] [[Category:Clans in the Shikoku Region]] [[Category:Clans in the Kanto Region]] [[Category:Kume District (Ehime Prefecture)]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大伴氏}} * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞}} * {{ill|Kume District|ja|久米郡}} * {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] （{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] （{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大伴氏}} * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞}} * {{ill|Kume District|ja|久米郡}} * （武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁== See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大伴氏}} * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞}} * {{ill|Kume District|ja|久米郡}} * According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also! == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造}} * {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大伴氏}} * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞}} * {{ill|Kume District|ja|久米郡}} * {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''{{ill|Shinsen Seishiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume|ja|久米}} |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別}}（{{ill|Tenshin|ja|天神}}） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = {{ill|Heishi|ja|平氏}} (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. {{ill|Fujiwara Northern Family|ja|藤原北家}} (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏}} (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] '''久米氏'''（くめうじ）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]の一つ。 == 久米直 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏
（久米直） |画像 = |氏姓 = 久米直 |始祖 = 味耳命（また味日命とも） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天津神・国津神|天神]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = [[山部氏|山部]][[連]] }} 古代日本における軍事氏族の一つで、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』では[[高魂命|高御魂命]]の8世の孫である味耳命（うましみみのみこと）の後裔とも、[[神魂命]]の8世の孫である味日命（うましひのみこと）の後裔とも伝わり、[[久米部]]（「くめべ」と読む。来目部とも表記することもある）の[[伴造]]氏族（[[久味国造]]）。 『[[日本書紀]]』神代下天孫降臨章1書には、[[大伴氏]]の遠祖の[[天忍日命]]が、来目部の遠祖である天槵津大来目（[[天津久米命]]）を率いて[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]を先導して天降ったと記されており、『新撰姓氏録』左京神別中の大伴宿禰条にも同様の記述がある。このことから、久米[[直 (姓)|直]]・久米部は大伴氏の配下にあって軍事的役割を有していたと考えられている。 ただ、『[[古事記]]』には天忍日命と天津久米命の2人が太刀・弓矢などを持って[[天孫降臨]]に供奉したとあり、大伴氏と久米氏を対等の立場として扱っており、[[神武東征]]において[[道臣命]]と[[大久米命]]の間にも記紀で同様の相違が認められる。すなわち、久米部を統轄する一族がある時点で衰えたため、大伴氏がかわりに久米部を管理するようになったということである{{Sfn|坂本ほか|1994|pp=382-383}}。『古事記』の伝承は、その古い形を伝えるものだったのかも知れない。 また、神武天皇東征説話に見える[[久米歌|来目歌]]、戦闘歌舞の代表といえる[[久米舞]]は、久米氏・久米部の性格を考える上で重要である{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2158}}。 === 由来諸説 === クメの原義に関する考察は、古くは[[本居宣長]]説として、「人の目の円く大きく、くるくるしたるゆえ」から始まり（以下、後述論文を参考）、クメ＝クミ（組）説、「クメベは軍隊のムレ（群れ）」説（[[橘守部]]、[[飯田武郷]]）、クメ＝クマ（肥）であり、「肥人つまり異民族の部民」説（[[喜田貞吉]]）、クメ＝クベ（垣）で、「宮廷の御垣」説（[[折口信夫]]）、クメ＝西南島々の地名説、「大伴氏が元々来目であり、大伴の部民」説（[[高橋富雄]]）が挙げられる（後述論文 p.322.）。 [[上田正昭]]は、本居説は論外であり、クベ＝垣説には注意を引くものがあるとした上で、「久米歌から考察するに、[[宇陀郡|宇陀]]の高城の防御を施した垣の内の集団生活にこそ、久米集団の本源的様相があり、元は山人で、南大和の王権に直属するようになり、御県の神が祀られるようになった」とする上田正昭「戦闘歌舞の伝統」（上田正昭編 『論集 日本文化の起源 第2巻 日本史』平凡社、 1971年）pp.322 - 323.。 == 久米臣 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏
（久米臣） |画像 = |氏姓 = 久米臣 |始祖 = |出自 = [[春日氏#和珥氏族（春日臣）|春日臣]] |氏祖 = |種別 = [[皇別]] |本貫 = [[大和国]][[高市郡]]久米郷 |人物 = |後裔 = }} [[大和国]][[高市郡]]久米郷より起こる。[[春日氏#和珥氏族（春日臣）|春日氏]]の傍流{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2158}}。 また、[[蘇我稲目]]の四男・[[蘇我刀名]]の子・[[久米薬子]]を祖とする久米臣も存在する倉本一宏『蘇我氏　古代豪族の興亡』（中央公論新社、2015年）。 {{-}} == 尾張国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = 久米氏 |画像 = |氏姓 = 久米 |始祖 = 八甕命 |出自 = 久米直 |氏祖 = |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天神]]） |本貫 = [[大和国]][[高市郡]]久米郷 |人物 = |後裔 = }} 久米氏の族人七掬脛の子・八甕命の流れ。氷上神社祠官であるといわれる{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2159}}。 {{-}} == 武蔵国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]][[村山氏|村山支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[武蔵国]][[入間郡]]久米庄 |根拠地= 武蔵国入間郡久米庄 |人物= |支流= }} [[武蔵国]][[入間郡]][[久米庄]]より起こる。[[本姓]]を[[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]]とする[[村山氏]]の流れという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2160}}。 {{-}} == 常陸国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= 1.[[藤原北家]][[藤原秀郷|秀郷流]][[小野崎氏|小野崎氏流]]
2.[[清和源氏]][[源義光|義光流]][[佐竹氏|佐竹支流]] |家祖= [[佐竹義武|久米義武]] |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[常陸国]][[久慈郡]]久米郷 |根拠地= 常陸国久慈郡久米郷 |人物= |支流= }} [[常陸国]]に[[久慈郡]]久米郷より起こる久米氏がいた。[[藤原北家]][[小野崎氏]]流があり、小野崎通室（筑前入道）の代に、男子がなく同族の[[小貫氏]]一族の[[小貫頼重]]（[[和田昭為]]の子、[[小貫頼久]]の養子）の子の通治を養子に迎えた。後に[[清和源氏]][[佐竹氏]]の当主の[[佐竹義治]]の子[[佐竹義武|久米義武]]がその名跡を継ぐも、[[佐竹義知 (山入佐竹氏)|山入義知]]の攻撃で討たれると、弟の[[佐竹義信|義信]]がその後を継いだという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2161}}。 なお、[[幕末]]の[[水戸藩]][[家老]] [[安島帯刀]]の妻は、通治の末裔ともされる[[久米長重|久米新七郎長重]]の娘であり、久米氏との間に[[御三卿]]筆頭 [[一橋徳川家]][[中老]]となった長女、水戸藩定江戸小姓 [[立原朴二郎]]妻となる二女、[[安島七郎太郎|安島七郎太郎信義]]、[[安島七郎三郎]]などの子があった[[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻8.5丁、145丁。 {{-}} == 出羽国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（出羽国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[藤原氏]][[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂|武智麻呂系]][[二階堂氏|二階堂支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[出羽国]]山本郡（現[[仙北郡]]）[[飯詰村 (秋田県)|飯詰]] |根拠地= 出羽国仙北郡飯詰 |人物= |支流= }} [[出羽国]]山本郡飯詰郷より起こった久米氏は、領主としては飯詰氏を名乗ることもあり、[[本姓]][[藤原氏]]、[[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂]]の子孫を称し、[[二階堂氏]]の分かれであるといわれている。[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の内乱期に出羽国に避難した一族が土着したと考えられる。[[曹洞宗]]の高僧[[道叟道愛]]も久米一族と伝わる。また、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、隣村[[六郷町 (秋田県)|六郷]]を本拠とした[[六郷氏]]も同族とみられ、久米姓は飯詰・六郷の所在する現在の[[美郷町 (秋田県)|美郷町]]やその周辺に濃密に分布し、また、六郷氏が出羽国[[由利郡]][[本荘藩]]の大名となったところから、その[[家臣]]として[[本荘市|本荘]]（現在の[[由利本荘市]]）へ随行した者、地元に残って[[秋田藩]][[家老]]渋江氏[[家臣]]の手前[[給人]]になった者r0403_katuhirajinja-sekizokomainu_1.pdfや[[飯詰村]]の[[豪農]]になった者[http://namahage.is.akita-u.ac.jp/monogatari/show_detail.php?serial_no=6152 与惣左エ門神社（よそうざえもんじんじゃ)]もおり、現在では[[秋田県]]南部から中部にかけて広く分布している。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬年]]、[[三上参次]]監修|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157-2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|ref=harv}} {{フリーアクセス}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|authorlink=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == 関連項目 == * [[天久米命]] * [[久味国造]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[久米舞]] * [[久米郡]] * [[垪和]] {{DEFAULTSORT:くめし}} [[Category:久米氏|!]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] [[Category:神別]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[久米氏]] to [[Kume clan]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

（常陸国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= 1.[[藤原北家]][[藤原秀郷|秀郷流]][[小野崎氏|小野崎氏流]]
2.[[清和源氏]][[源義光|義光流]][[佐竹氏|佐竹支流]] |家祖= [[佐竹義武|久米義武]] |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[常陸国]][[久慈郡]]久米郷 |根拠地= 常陸国久慈郡久米郷 |人物= |支流= }} [[常陸国]]に[[久慈郡]]久米郷より起こる久米氏がいた。[[藤原北家]][[小野崎氏]]流があり、小野崎通室（筑前入道）の代に、男子がなく同族の[[小貫氏]]一族の[[小貫頼重]]（[[和田昭為]]の子、[[小貫頼久]]の養子）の子の通治を養子に迎えた。後に[[清和源氏]][[佐竹氏]]の当主の[[佐竹義治]]の子[[佐竹義武|久米義武]]がその名跡を継ぐも、[[佐竹義知 (山入佐竹氏)|山入義知]]の攻撃で討たれると、弟の[[佐竹義信|義信]]がその後を継いだという{{Sfn|太田|1934|p=2161}}。 なお、[[幕末]]の[[水戸藩]][[家老]] [[安島帯刀]]の妻は、通治の末裔ともされる[[久米長重|久米新七郎長重]]の娘であり、久米氏との間に[[御三卿]]筆頭 [[一橋徳川家]][[中老]]となった長女、水戸藩定江戸小姓 [[立原朴二郎]]妻となる二女、[[安島七郎太郎|安島七郎太郎信義]]、[[安島七郎三郎]]などの子があった[[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻8.5丁、145丁。 {{-}} == 出羽国の久米氏 == {{日本の氏族 |家名= 久米氏
（出羽国久米氏） |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= [[藤原氏]][[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂|武智麻呂系]][[二階堂氏|二階堂支流]] |家祖= |種別= [[武家]] |出身地= [[出羽国]]山本郡（現[[仙北郡]]）[[飯詰村 (秋田県)|飯詰]] |根拠地= 出羽国仙北郡飯詰 |人物= |支流= }} [[出羽国]]山本郡飯詰郷より起こった久米氏は、領主としては飯詰氏を名乗ることもあり、[[本姓]][[藤原氏]]、[[藤原南家|南家]][[藤原武智麻呂]]の子孫を称し、[[二階堂氏]]の分かれであるといわれている。[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の内乱期に出羽国に避難した一族が土着したと考えられる。[[曹洞宗]]の高僧[[道叟道愛]]も久米一族と伝わる。また、[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、隣村[[六郷町 (秋田県)|六郷]]を本拠とした[[六郷氏]]も同族とみられ、久米姓は飯詰・六郷の所在する現在の[[美郷町 (秋田県)|美郷町]]やその周辺に濃密に分布し、また、六郷氏が出羽国[[由利郡]][[本荘藩]]の大名となったところから、その[[家臣]]として[[本荘市|本荘]]（現在の[[由利本荘市]]）へ随行した者、地元に残って[[秋田藩]][[家老]]渋江氏[[家臣]]の手前[[給人]]になった者r0403_katuhirajinja-sekizokomainu_1.pdfや[[飯詰村]]の[[豪農]]になった者[http://namahage.is.akita-u.ac.jp/monogatari/show_detail.php?serial_no=6152 与惣左エ門神社（よそうざえもんじんじゃ)]もおり、現在では[[秋田県]]南部から中部にかけて広く分布している。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬年]]、[[三上参次]]監修|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157-2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|ref=harv}} {{フリーアクセス}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|authorlink=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == 関連項目 == * [[天久米命]] * [[久味国造]] * [[大伴氏]] * [[久米舞]] * [[久米郡]] * [[垪和]] {{DEFAULTSORT:くめし}} [[Category:久米氏|!]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:岡山県の歴史]] [[Category:神別]]

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]]

According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. （武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]]

revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]]
