According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]]

（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[en:Kume clan]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[Musashi Province]] [[Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:近畿地方の氏族]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:久米氏]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:四国地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:久米氏]] into [[Category:Kume Clan]] via bot
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:Clans of Shikoku Region]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:関東地方の氏族]]

{{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{日本の氏族 (古代氏族) |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:久米郡 (愛媛県)]] [[Category:Clans of Shikoku Region]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} '''Kume Clan''' (久米氏, くめうじ) is one of the clans of Japan. == Kume Naoshi == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |始祖 = {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = |人物 = |後裔 = {{ill|Yamabe Clan|ja|山部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamabe Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山部 (品部)}}連 }} In ancient Japan, the Kume Clan was one of the military clans. According to the ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', they are said to be descendants either of {{ill|Takamikoto no Mikoto|ja|高御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamikoto no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s 8th‐generation grandson, {{ill|Umashimimi no Mikoto|ja|味耳命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashimimi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also called {{ill|Umashihime no Mikoto|ja|味日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashihime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or of the 8th‐generation grandson of {{ill|Shinkon no Mikoto|ja|神魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkon no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}. They are associated with the Kumebe (read “くめべ”, sometimes also written as "来目部") clan of the Banzo (【伴造】) type (【久味国造】). == Kume Omi == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（{{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Kume Omi|ja|久米臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = |出自 = {{ill|Kasuga Clan|ja|春日氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Clan|qq=}} (specifically, the branch known as the Wagetsu of the Kasuga line) |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōbetsu|qq=}} |本貫 = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The branch known as Kume Omi (久米臣) originated from the Kume-gō of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] and is considered a branch of the Kasuga clan’s Wagetsu lineage. == Kume Clan of Owari Province == {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}} |image = |氏姓 = [[:en:Kume (disambiguation)|Kume]] |始祖 = {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |出自 = {{ill|Kume Naoshi|ja|久米直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Naoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 久米氏}} |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Kamibetsu|ja|神別|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamibetsu|qq=}}（[[:en:Tenjin|Tenshin]]） |本貫 = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] [[Takaichi District]] Kume-gō |人物 = |後裔 = }} The Owari branch of the Kume Clan is said to descend from the lineage of {{ill|Hachimitsu no Mikoto|ja|八甕命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachimitsu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the descendant of a clan member known as “Shichiku-ketsu,” who is reputed to have served as the chief priest at {{ill|Hijō Shrine|ja|氷上神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hijō Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Kume Clan of Musashi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（武蔵国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[:en:Taira clan|Heishi]] (specifically, as a branch of the Kanmu Heishi and the Murayama subgroup) |家祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] Kume-shō |根拠地 = Musashi Province, Iruma District, Kume-shō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Musashi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-shō in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] [[:en:Iruma District|Iruma District]] and is considered a branch of the Murayama line, which claims descent from the Kanmu Heishi. == Kume Clan of Hitachi Province == {{日本の氏族 |家名 = {{ill|Kume Clan|ja|久米氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Clan|qq=}}
（常陸国久米氏） |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = 1. [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Fujiwara Northern Family]] (Hidesato lineage, also associated with the Onozaki clan)
2. [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] (Yoritomo lineage, with a branch related to the Satake clan) |家祖 = {{ill|Satake Yoshitake|ja|佐竹義武|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satake Yoshitake|qq=}} (Kume Yoshitake) |種別 = {{ill|Buke|ja|武家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buke|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |出身地 = [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] [[Kuzugun]] Kume-gō |根拠地 = Hitachi Province, Kuzugun, Kume-gō |人物 = |支流 = }} The Hitachi branch of the Kume Clan originated from Kume-gō in [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]]’s [[Kuzugun]] and existed in two major lines—one stemming from the Fujiwara Northern Family (Hidesato/Onozaki lineage) and the other from the Seiwa Genji (Satake lineage). During the Nanboku-chō period, a branch of the clan that sought refuge in Hitachi became indigenous; additionally, during the Sengoku period, members of the Kume clan were said to have been retainers of the Satake clan when it became feudal lords in Hitachi, and many became prosperous farmers in areas corresponding to present-day southern Akita Prefecture (such as Misato Town) and surrounding regions. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|last=太田|first=亮|author-link=太田亮|others=[[上田萬]]、[[三上参次]] (eds.)|chapter=久米　クメ|pages=2157–2162|volume=第2|date=1934|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/172}}|ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=坂本太郎ほか校注|author-link=坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|date=1994-9|volume=第1巻|title=日本書紀|series=[[岩波文庫]] 黄4-1|publisher=[[岩波書店]]|isbn=9784003000410|ref={{SfnRef|坂本ほか|1994}}}} * [[財団法人]] 水府明徳会彰考館蔵『水府系纂』第45巻 8.5丁、145丁 == See Also == * {{ill|Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|ja|天久米命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkumegumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津久米命}} * {{ill|Kumeguni no Mikoto|ja|久味国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumeguni no Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo Clan]] * {{ill|Kume Mai|ja|久米舞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Mai|qq=}} * [[:en:Kume District, Okayama|Kume District]] * {{ill|Mutsuwa|ja|垪和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsuwa|qq=}} == Categories == [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|久米氏|version=100754220|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Kume clan]] [[ja:久米氏]] [[sn:ume clan]] [[Category:Kume Clan]] [[Category:Kume District (Ehime)]] [[Category:Clans of Shikoku Region]] [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Clans by region]] [[Category:Clans of Kantō Region]]
